content:
Character: Helena Kruger
Perception: Helena is slender, pale, precise in her movements — she reminds me of certain Japanese women I knew in university, not in appearance but in the way she holds herself with quiet discipline. She knocked on my door because she smelled my dashi. Most people would have complained. She watched me cook in silence and only asked one question: 'How do you know the kombu is ready?' A good question. She returned my Rilke book with a page marked — a poem about solitude. I understood. She is a careful person. I think she sees me as a craftsman she respects. I see her as a neighbor who does not require translation.

Perception: Helena is slender, pale, precise in her movements — she reminds me of certain Japanese women I knew in university, not in appearance but in the way she holds herself with quiet discipline. She knocked on my door because she smelled my dashi. Most people would have complained. She watched me cook in silence and only asked one question: 'How do you know the kombu is ready?' A good question. She returned my Rilke book with a page marked — a poem about solitude. I understood. She is a careful person. I think she sees me as a craftsman she respects. I see her as a neighbor who does not require translation.
